title: [Houndoom] Battle Guide
summary: A [Dark]/[Fire] special [sweeper] that uses [flash-fire] as a pivot into [nasty-plot], then deletes special walls with [dark-pulse] and [flamethrower].
strengths: [flash-fire] lets it switch into [Fire] moves and hit back at +50%
A [sing]le [nasty-plot] pushes effective Special Attack past 220
weaknesses: 2x weak to [Fighting], [Ground], [Rock], and [Water] — plenty of [revenge] killers
50 Defense / 80 Special Defense folds to almost any neutral hit
Base 95 Speed is not fast enough to outrun every meta [sweeper]

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [Dark] STAB with solid power and flinch utility
+2 Special Attack sets up the full sweep line
Cove[rage] against [Fairy] switch-ins like Azumarill or Tapu Koko

recommendedItems.reason: Guarantees a turn even into a priority hit

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Houndoom] is a [Dark]/[Fire] special attacker whose Champions MA niche is the [flash-fire] pivot into [nasty-plot]. The ability turns any predicted [Fire] move into a free 50% power boost, and once one [nasty-plot] goes up the entire back line of the opponent starts falling to [dark-pulse] and [flamethrower] double-STAB.

## Strengths
[flash-fire] is the reason to run this over other special [Fire] attackers — it gives [Houndoom] a free pivot slot and stacks on top of the 110 Special Attack. [Dark] and [Fire] as a STAB pair has almost no shared resistances — [Psychic], [Ghost], [Grass], [Ice], [Bug], and [Steel] all take neutral or super-effective damage from one side or the other. One [nasty-plot] puts effective Special Attack above 220, enough that even bulky Aegislash lines get 2HKO'd. [sludge-bomb] patches the one real hole, the [Fairy] switch-in.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Dark]/[Fire] is 2x weak to [Fighting], [Ground], [Rock], and [Water], which lines up with most of the meta's common [revenge] killers — Swampert, Excadrill, and Keldeo all beat [Houndoom] in a direct matchup. Base 50 Defense and 80 Special Defense means even neutral hits often 2HKO. Priority users with [aqua-jet], [mach-punch], or [bullet-punch] can [revenge] the sash. [stealth-rock] chip also deletes 25% HP on every entry, which is lethal to the [focus-sash] plan.

## Recommended Moveset
[dark-pulse] is the main STAB — reliable power plus a flinch chance. [flamethrower] is picked over [fire]-blast because accuracy matters when the set is trying to string together kills after a plot. [nasty-plot] is the whole win condition and takes the third slot. [sludge-bomb] handles [Fairy] switch-ins like Tapu Koko or Azumarill that otherwise wall the [Dark] STAB. Swap [sludge-bomb] for [sucker-punch] if the team lacks priority.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default item, guaranteeing a [nasty-plot] turn even against a priority lead. The ability is [flash-fire] — the pivot potential is worth more than the raw power of [solar-power] in the base form. Tera [Fire] maximizes STAB [flamethrower] output, while Tera [Dark] leans on the [dark-pulse] line for late-game sweep confirmation.

## Teammates & Synergy
A [stealth-rock] remover ([defog] or [rapid-spin] user) is effectively mandatory because [Houndoom] loses 25% on every entry otherwise. Defensive [Fighting] and [Ground] answers (Latias, Gliscor) buffer the exact weaknesses that [revenge] [Houndoom], and a [Water]-type removal partner ([Grass] or [Electric] [sweeper]) clears the way for it to click plot safely. [fake-out] support is the cleanest way to buy the setup turn.

## Play Patterns
Switch [Houndoom] in on a predicted [Fire] move to activate [flash-fire], then click [nasty-plot] the same turn the opponent swaps. After the boost is up, click [dark-pulse] for safe damage and save [flamethrower] for when the [flash-fire] buff is still active. Tera only on the turn a super-effective move is telegraphed, or when [dark-pulse] is confirming a KO — those are the two moments the Tera actually wins the game.
